rationale: The query's core method is a self-consistent mean-field solution for the critical temperature/coupling in a binary-state model (Ising/neuronal) on a network with arbitrary degree distribution, governed by the moments of P(k). The candidate uses deep learning (CNN + bidirectional LSTM) to classify sleep stages from raw EEG, with no shared machinery—its method is supervised feature extraction and temporal modeling, not a self-consistent mean-field closure. The object differs (neuronal activity on structural networks vs. sleep-stage EEG signals), so object_difference is true, but method_coherence is false.
